name: Celery-Apple Salad with Currants
recipeInstructions:
1. If the currants are hard, cover them with warm water and set aside to soften while you prepare the other ingredients.
- After about 10 minutes, drain the currants and squeeze out the excess water.
2. Separate the celery stalks and peel away any tough outer layers.
3. Slice the celery into thin pieces (straight or diagonal).
4. Cut the apples into quarters or sixths if they are large; slice them thinly crosswise.
5. Finely chop the celery leaves and parsley.
6. Crack the walnuts into small pieces.
7. Combine the prepared celery, apples, currants, celery leaves, parsley, and walnuts in a bowl.
8. Toss the mixture with just enough walnut oil to lightly coat all ingredients.
9. Add lemon juice (or vinegar) to taste, then season with salt and freshly ground pepper. Toss again to ensure everything is evenly seasoned.
